title: China weighs silicon curtain around sought-after AI models
friendlyTitle: China may limit access to some AI models
summary: Reuters reports that Beijing has discussed restricting overseas access to Chinese AI models while DeepSeek is developing its own AI chip. The story frames this alongside U.S. controls on frontier model access and growing business dependence on lower-cost Chinese open-source models.
friendlySummary: Reuters says China is considering tighter rules on overseas access to Chinese AI models, while DeepSeek is also working on its own AI chip.
whyItMatters: Model access is becoming an operational risk, not just a policy debate. Companies using Chinese open models for cost and performance may need contingency plans if access rules tighten or geopolitical controls reshape the AI supply chain.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Businesses that rely on open Chinese AI models may need backup plans if access changes.
